Wuxi Sunlit Science & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1289) ) has shared an announcement.

Wuxi Sunlit Science and Technology Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Wuxi, where shareholders will review the board and supervisory reports, the 2025 annual report, and the audited consolidated financial statements. They will also vote on the 2025 profit distribution plan and confirm fees for PricewaterhouseCoopers.

Shareholders are further asked to approve the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as external auditor, authorize the purchase of directors’ liability insurance, and adopt amendments to the Articles of Association. The notice also sets key share register closure dates that determine investor eligibility to attend the AGM and receive the proposed final dividend for the 2025 financial year.

More about Wuxi Sunlit Science & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Wuxi Sunlit Science and Technology Company Limited is a joint stock company incorporated in mainland China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1289. The group operates through subsidiaries and is headquartered in Wuxi, Jiangsu Province, highlighting its base in one of China’s key industrial and economic development zones.

The latest update is out from Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company ( (HK:0468) ).

Greatview Aseptic Packaging has disclosed key findings from an independent internal control review mandated as part of Hong Kong Stock Exchange resumption guidance for its suspended shares. The company engaged BT Corporate Governance Limited to assess its internal controls and report on adequacy, remedial actions and compliance across core financial and operational processes.

The review covered revenue, procurement, inventory, HR and payroll, capital expenditure, operating expenses, financial reporting, transaction approval and IT controls at the parent company and three major mainland subsidiaries. It examined the period from January 1, 2025 to January 31, 2026, with a follow‑up review in February 2026 on implemented remedial measures, signalling ongoing efforts to address issues previously raised by shareholder-triggered investigations and to progress toward trading resumption.

More about Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed manufacturer of aseptic packaging, supplying carton packaging solutions for liquid food producers. The group operates through subsidiaries in Beijing, Shandong and Inner Mongolia, focusing on the Chinese market and complying with Hong Kong Listing Rules as a public issuer.

Digital China Holdings ( (HK:0861) ) just unveiled an update.

Digital China Holdings has reclassified a series of existing commercial dealings as continuing connected transactions after Digital China Group (DCG) increased its shareholding above 10% on April 16, 2026, making DCG a substantial shareholder and connected person under Hong Kong listing rules. Brainhole Technology Limited ( (HK:2203) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Brainhole Technology Limited has executed a further disposal of 3,600 Nebius shares in the open market on 16 April 2026 for about US$0.6 million, bringing the total consideration from all Nebius share disposals within 12 months to roughly US$4.0 million. The aggregation of these disposals under Hong Kong Listing Rules reclassifies the latest sale as a major transaction, triggering reporting, announcement, circular and shareholder approval requirements that the company has satisfied through written consent from its controlling shareholder, Yoho Bravo Limited, thereby avoiding the need for an extraordinary general meeting.

This move continues Brainhole Technology’s gradual exit or downsizing of its Nebius position and may signal a rebalancing of its investment portfolio, potentially freeing capital for other uses while maintaining regulatory compliance. With a circular to be dispatched for information purposes only, minority shareholders are informed of the transaction but do not influence its approval, underscoring the decisive role of the controlling shareholder in shaping the company’s investment decisions and governance processes.

More about Brainhole Technology Limited

Brainhole Technology Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2203. The company actively manages a portfolio of listed securities as part of its capital allocation strategy, including investments in Nebius, and is subject to Hong Kong’s Listing Rules for major transactions and shareholder approvals.

An announcement from Hope Life International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1683) ) is now available.

Hope Life International Holdings Ltd. has announced a series of boardroom changes effective 16 April 2026, including the resignation of executive director Li Ka Chun Gordon and independent non-executive director Chan Wai Yan, who also stepped down as chair of the audit committee and member of the nomination committee, both citing other business commitments and no disagreement with the board. The board emphasized that there are no other matters related to the resignations that need to be brought to shareholders’ or the exchange’s attention, suggesting a smooth transition rather than a signal of internal dispute. With Pau’s background at major listed corporations and a global audit firm, the reshuffle is likely aimed at reinforcing the company’s financial governance and committee leadership, which may reassure investors concerned about continuity in audit and nomination oversight.

Cross-Harbour (Holdings) ( (HK:0032) ) has provided an update.

The Cross-Harbour (Holdings) Limited has agreed to dispose of its entire 1.0714% limited partnership interest in a Cayman Islands exempted fund focused on early- and growth-stage consumer-related and prosumer-related e-commerce, retail and internet services companies. The interest, held via indirect wholly owned subsidiary MWH, represents a total capital commitment of US$15 million, including invested capital, recallable distributions and outstanding commitments.

Cathay Pacific Airways announced upcoming board changes effective after its annual general meeting on 13 May 2026, with non-executive directors Sun Yuquan set to retire under the company’s articles and Wang Mingyuan resigning due to his own retirement, both stating they have no disagreement with the board. The latest update is out from Onewo, Inc. Class H ( (HK:2602) ).

Onewo Inc. has called its 2026 annual general meeting for May 8, to be held online, where shareholders will vote on standard annual matters, including approval of the 2025 annual report, board and financial reports, the profit distribution plan, and the reappointment of the auditor. The company is also seeking a special mandate for its board to issue up to 20% additional H shares over the next year, giving directors broad authority over pricing, timing, intermediaries and related documentation, a move that could provide capital-raising flexibility but may also dilute existing holders and reshape its capital structure.

If approved, the new mandate would allow the board, or a delegated chairman, to adjust the registered capital and amend the articles of association following any fresh H-share issuance. data.title: Zhongmiao Holdings Partners on Intelligent Fire-Safety Robots for Warehousing Sector
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Zhongmiao Holdings (Qingdao) Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1471) ) has shared an update.

Zhongmiao Holdings (Qingdao) Co., Ltd., an insurance technology-driven risk management group in China, focuses on combining insurance distribution with advanced systems to improve safety in warehousing and logistics. Its self-developed Anshu Intelligent Prevention System is designed to shift warehouse risk control toward intelligent, data-driven and intervenable management, helping clients enhance intrinsic safety standards while reducing costs and operational uncertainty.

The company has entered a long-term strategic cooperation with the University of Emergency Management and Beijing Huizhi Zhong’an Technology to jointly develop an industry-leading intelligent robot system for fire safety inspection in large-scale warehouses. Leveraging academic research, robotics expertise and Zhongmiao’s risk-management platform, the partnership aims to upgrade the Anshu system with autonomous hazard identification and emergency response, aligning with China’s work safety modernization agenda and expanding the Group’s insurance technology services to strengthen safety, efficiency and risk prevention in the warehousing and logistics industry.

By tackling key technical challenges in intelligent inspection and proactive intervention, the collaboration is expected to move risk management from passive response to proactive and precise control. This initiative could reinforce Zhongmiao’s position in the risk-reduction segment of the insurance technology market and deliver tangible benefits for warehousing enterprises and logistics operators through enhanced fire safety and reduced social risks.

More about Zhongmiao Holdings (Qingdao) Co., Ltd. Class H

Zhongmiao Holdings (Qingdao) Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based insurance technology group focused on risk reduction, combining insurance distribution with proprietary insurance technology. The company has developed its Anshu Intelligent Prevention System to transform warehouse risk management from a reactive, opaque process into an intelligent, closed-loop platform that enables perception, quantification and intervention in logistics and warehousing safety risks.

Positioned at the intersection of insurance, technology and industrial safety, the Group targets high-risk warehousing and logistics scenarios, seeking to set best-practice standards for risk management in China. Its solutions aim to enhance safety, lower operating costs and improve efficiency for warehousing enterprises, while strengthening risk prevention and control capabilities across the broader logistics sector.

data.title: Graphex Group Wins Extension to Meet Hong Kong Board Governance Rules
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Graphex Group Limited ( (HK:6128) ).

Graphex Group Limited has disclosed that, following the death of independent non-executive director (INED) Ms. Tam and the resignation of INED Mr. Wang, it still falls short of Hong Kong listing rules on the minimum number of independent directors, audit committee members and gender diversity on its board. The company recently appointed one new executive director and one new INED, but these changes were insufficient to fully restore compliance because a suitable female INED candidate could not yet be confirmed.

The Stock Exchange has granted Graphex an extension of the grace period from 9 April 2026 to 30 June 2026 for the appointment of a female INED, allowing more time to complete identification, selection and nomination procedures. The company says it will use its best efforts to fill the remaining INED vacancy and thereby re-comply with rules on board independence, committee composition and gender diversity, a step that is important for meeting regulatory standards and maintaining investor confidence in its governance framework.

More about Graphex Group Limited

Graphex Group Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and focused on materials for electric vehicles, operating within the broader new energy and advanced materials sector. Its board comprises executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting typical governance structures for companies in this industry and market segment.

The company’s operations and market positioning are tied closely to compliance with Hong Kong listing rules, which govern board composition, audit oversight and gender diversity. These governance requirements influence investor perception, regulatory risk and the firm’s ability to access capital markets and maintain its listing status over time.

Fosun International ( (HK:0656) ) has issued an announcement.

Fosun International has highlighted the first-quarter 2026 performance of its Shanghai-listed subsidiary Yuyuan, which reported revenue of RMB 9.65 billion, up 9.87% year on year. Net profit attributable to shareholders surged more than threefold to RMB 156.97 million, with core profit excluding non-recurring items swinging from a loss to a profit, indicating a marked improvement in operating performance.

Despite stronger earnings, Yuyuan’s net cash flow from operating activities fell into negative territory at RMB -485.55 million, compared with a positive RMB 317.78 million a year earlier, while total assets and shareholder equity edged down slightly versus year-end 2025. The figures suggest that while profitability is recovering, cash generation and balance-sheet trends will be key areas for investors to monitor in assessing the sustainability of the turnaround.

data.title: Pacific Basin orders two more fuel-efficient Handysize bulkers for US$59.6 million
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pacific Basin Shipping ( (HK:2343) ) has shared an update.

Pacific Basin Shipping has agreed to acquire two additional Handysize newbuilding vessels from a shipyard seller for a total of about US$59.6 million, to be funded through cash reserves and/or bank borrowings, with delivery scheduled for the second half of 2028. The move follows a prior order for four similar Handysize vessels placed in December 2025, and collectively reflects the company’s ongoing strategy to renew and expand its fleet with fuel-efficient, open-hatch, logs-fitted ships that are expected to offer improved cargo flexibility and potentially higher earnings in the current newbuilding market.

Management said the pricing, specifications and timing of the new vessels were attractive relative to comparable newbuildings and second-hand tonnage, and that the contracts were struck on normal commercial terms after arm’s length negotiations. The additions, backed by performance and refund guarantees, are intended to strengthen Pacific Basin’s competitive position in Handysize bulk trades by increasing carrying capacity and enabling more triangulated trading patterns, which could support time-charter-equivalent outperformance over older standard designs.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2343) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pacific Basin Shipping stock, see the HK:2343 Stock Forecast page.

More about Pacific Basin Shipping

Pacific Basin Shipping is a Hong Kong-based dry bulk shipping company focused on Handysize and Supramax vessels that transport commodities worldwide. The group operates owned and chartered-in ships, targeting fuel-efficient, modern designs to enhance operational flexibility and time-charter-equivalent earnings in the global bulk logistics market.

Emperor Watch & Jewellery ( (HK:0887) ) has provided an announcement.

Emperor Watch & Jewellery has called its annual general meeting for 18 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will vote on the adoption of the 2025 audited financial statements, the declaration of a final dividend, the re-election of two directors, and the re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as independent auditor. The meeting will also seek shareholder approval to grant the board a general mandate to issue new shares up to 20% of the existing share capital and to buy back up to 10% of its own shares, measures that would give the company greater flexibility in managing its capital structure and potential future funding or share repurchase activities.

More about Emperor Watch & Jewellery

Emperor Watch & Jewellery Limited is a Hong Kong–incorporated retailer specialising in luxury timepieces and fine jewellery. The company targets mid- to high-end consumers, operating primarily in Hong Kong and other key Asian markets where demand for branded watches and jewellery is driven by tourism and affluent local customers.

data.description:

China Bohai Bank Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9668) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Bohai Bank announced that China’s National Financial Regulatory Administration has approved the qualification of Xie Kai as the bank’s chief information officer, formalizing an appointment originally disclosed in December 2025. His role became effective on April 13, 2026, and he concurrently serves as a member of the party committee and a vice president of the bank.

The confirmation of Xie Kai’s qualification strengthens the bank’s senior management structure in the critical area of information technology and digital operations, underscoring regulatory confidence in its leadership. This move may support China Bohai Bank’s efforts to enhance its technology governance and digital transformation capabilities, factors that are increasingly important for competitiveness and risk management in China’s banking sector.

More about China Bohai Bank Co., Ltd. Class H

China Bohai Bank Co., Ltd. is a joint stock commercial bank incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 9668. The bank operates within China’s financial services industry, offering a range of banking products and services to corporate and retail clients, with governance overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.
